danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Areas requiring combat or avoidance; threat to player health

danger_signals.visual_form: Castle architecture with trap-themed visual design elements
danger_signals.meaning: Ancient castle setting conveys danger through themed level design

danger_signals.visual_form: Edgar Ektor as recurring antagonist character with distinctive visual presence
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat moment; narrative danger culmination

safety_signals.meaning: Health/ability restoration; temporary invulnerability or enhanced combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden passages and concealed zones in level design
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones containing extra lives, letters (A-E-R-O), and bonus resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Level terminus location at end of each act
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; completion of challenge segment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Acrobat bat character with drill jump ability
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Museum of Horrors setting; ancient castle aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ektor as identifiable antagonist; Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel as henchman
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy creature designs throughout castle levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Museum of Horrors (Ektor's domain); ancient castle setting suggests his magical/dark theme

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Separate character with own game (Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel series spin-off)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Henchman identity distinct from generic enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears as rescue agent in narrative; associated with Ektor's Plan B

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aero explores interior of Ektor's Museum of Horrors; discovers magician's box leading to ancient castle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aero investigates environment; discovers new threat through environmental interaction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient castle environment represents new domain/stronghold; eight-world structure suggests escalating threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zero saves Ektor; prepares countermeasure; enemy encounters increase in complexity across eight worlds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight worlds with three acts each (except final world); significantly longer levels than original game with expanded secret areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Persistent enemy types; increased enemy density; hidden mechanics (four hidden letters per act spelling A-E-R-O)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: PAL version: one level contains framerate-dependent cannon mechanic visually rendered but functionally broken
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NTSC version: cannon behaves correctly; PAL version requires level select to bypass unbeatable segment

environmental_storytelling.example: Museum of Horrors establishes Ektor's character through architecture; ancient castle conveys magical/dark narrative context
Eight worlds with escalating complexity signal increasing threat; final world (single act) suggests climactic confrontation
Four hidden letters per act spelling 'AERO' creates metanarrative layer; secret areas reward exploration, reinforcing player agency
Zero's rescue of Ektor shown through story progression; Plan B preparation informs enemy encounter design
SGI-rendered backgrounds create 'dark theme' atmosphere; enhanced graphics show technological advancement over first game, suggesting evolved threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Drill jump ability (diagonal up/down, now including straight-down attack); star-throwing combat; food collection for points
Searches for act exits; collects hidden letters; navigates secret areas
Instructs henchman Zero; prepares Plan B; represents ongoing threat across eight worlds
Saves Ektor mid-fall; executes boss-level interventions; represents escalated opposition
Positioned in level geometry; require drill-jump combat or star attacks; increase in frequency/complexity across eight worlds
Appear in open areas and secret zones; restore health or enable special abilities; create risk/reward exploration incentives

notes: Aero the Acro-Bat 2 uses minimalist faction communication through protagonist vs. antagonist binary (Aero vs. Ektor/Zero). World-state is communicated primarily through environmental theming (Museum → Castle) rather than explicit UI. Visual danger is conveyed through creature design and level architecture rather than explicit threat indicators. The game's 'dark theme' is achieved through rendering technology (SGI backgrounds) and level design rather than narrative dialogue. Regional differences (PAL cannon bug) represent unintended world-state variance rather than deliberate design communication. The hidden AERO letter system creates metanarrative communication layer separate from main threat/safety signaling.
